title: Korean Bowtie Pasta Dobu-Jjigae
description: Soft, gooey Korean-style pasta dish, perfect for a quick and easy dinner. Serve with a side of kimchi for added flavor.
instructions: Cook the bowtie pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the toasted sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the gochujang, soy sauce, and brown sugar to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Add the cubed tofu to the skillet and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Stir in the chopped green onions and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooked bowtie pasta to the skillet and toss to combine with the sauce.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 2-3 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
Use a spatula to shape the pasta into small bowties, similar to nigiri.
Serve the Korean bowtie pasta hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
Nigiri-shape the pasta by gently pressing the bowtie into a small, compact shape.
